payload.notes: Final E15 story; enforce fresh-checkout restore in CI and keep snapshot replacement explicit, compare-and-swap guarded, and outside ordinary tasks.
payload.title: CI Rebuild Gate And Snapshot Compaction

payload.evidence: Fresh source checkout with no writable DB or prebuilt CLI reconstructs through US-119 from tracked state. Linux and Windows CI bootstrap before validation. Snapshot replacement is verified and compare-and-swap guarded. The complete pre-merge suite passes with 97 Rust tests.

payload.actions_taken: ["Added Linux and Windows missing-state bootstrap gates; added workflow contract checks; added a no-DB/no-binary fresh-source fixture; added compare-and-swap snapshot replacement and tests; updated SQLite and reproducible-state decisions; ran the complete pre-merge suite."]
payload.decisions_made: ["Make fresh tracked-state bootstrap an explicit CI prerequisite on Linux and Windows; require snapshot replacement compare-and-swap against the reviewed logical hash; keep incorporated JSONL and make retention a separate decision; never compact during ordinary tasks."]
payload.errors: ["The first fresh-copy fixture excluded both files named harness.db; fixed the fixture to restore the tracked baseline only","then the no-runtime-state bootstrap and full suite passed."]
payload.harness_friction: BSD tar treated a basename exclusion as matching the tracked baseline as well as the root writable database; the fresh-checkout fixture now restores only the tracked snapshot explicitly after excluding runtime state.
payload.notes: Platform proof is true because the CI workflow executes native Bash and PowerShell bootstrap paths and its ordering is contract-tested locally.
payload.task_summary: Added and proved the fresh-clone CI rebuild gate and guarded snapshot compaction procedure.
